Weight.title: Weigh Pigs with a Tape & Unit Converter
Weight.description: Estimate swine live weight using chest girth and body length tape measurements, or convert standard weight metrics.
Weight.bodyLengthDesc: Measure from the base of the ears to the base of the tail.
Weight.heartGirthDesc: Measure the circumference of the chest directly behind front legs.
Weight.measurementGuide: Measure Length (Point A) and Girth (Point B) as shown above.
Weight.enterPositiveValues: Enter positive heart girth and body length values to run live/carcass weight projection.
Weight.noPigs: No pigs found in your herd database.
Weight.noteBodyLength: Body Length: Measure from the base of the ears to the base of the tail making sure the tape is firm on the body and not loose (Point A).
Weight.noteHeartGirth: Heart Girth: Measure the thorax (just behind the front legs) making sure the tape is fit along the body and meets tightly (Point B).
Weight.noteSquarely: Ensure the pig is standing squarely on level ground for the most accurate result.
Weight.saveSuccess: Weight successfully saved to pig profile!
Weight.saveError: Error saving weight. Check your connection.
Weight.validWeightAlert: Please enter a valid weight in kg.
Weight.tapeEstimationLog: Tape calculation estimated weight: {weight} kg / {lbs} lbs (Girth: {girth} {unit}, Length: {length} {unit}).

Training.title: Quick Farming Tips
Training.noTipsFound: No tips found matching your search.
Training.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this training video tutorial?

tip6.title: Weaning Age for Smallholders
tip6.content: Wean piglets at 28 to 35 days instead of earlier. This allows piglets to grow stronger and adapt to solid feed using simple farm resources.

tip7.title: Gradual Separation Method
tip7.content: Reduce weaning stress by moving the sow out of the farrowing pen and leaving the piglets in their familiar environment for the first week.

tip8.title: Draft Prevention for Weaners
tip8.content: Block cold winds in the nursery pen by installing temporary curtains or wooden boards up to piglet-height (about 50cm).

tip11.title: Homemade Rehydration Solution
tip11.content: Feed a cheap, homemade electrolyte solution (5 tablespoons sugar, 1/2 tablespoon salt in 5 liters clean water) during the first 3 days of weaning to combat stress.

tip17.title: Clay Soil Mineral Supplement
tip17.content: Put a clean shovel of fresh, red clay soil (from a clean area) in the farrowing/weaning pen. Piglets eat it to get natural iron and minerals.

tip20.title: Agricultural Byproduct Boiling
tip20.content: Boil agricultural byproducts like yam peels, cassava peels, and potato vines to destroy natural toxins and improve digestibility.

tip21.title: Local Protein Sources
tip21.content: Supplement commercial feed by growing high-protein forage crops like Moringa, Azolla, or sweet potato leaves on the farm.

tip22.title: Gestation Feed Restriction
tip22.content: Limit gestating sows to 2-2.5 kg of feed per day to prevent them from becoming overweight, which causes difficult births.

tip26.title: Boiling Kitchen Waste
tip26.content: Boil hotel or kitchen food waste for 30 minutes to destroy African Swine Fever and Foot-and-Mouth disease viruses.

tip28.title: Charcoal for Digestion
tip28.content: Place small pieces of wood charcoal in grower pens. Pigs chew it to help bind stomach toxins and reduce diarrhea.

tip34.title: Hand Mating in Breeding Pen
tip34.content: Bring the sow in heat to the boar's pen for breeding, rather than letting the boar run loose in the sow herd.

tip35.title: Standing Heat Response
tip35.content: Test for heat by pressing your hands firmly on the sow's back; if she stands completely still and stiffens her ears, she is ready to mate.

tip39.title: DIY Crushed Piglet Protection
tip39.content: Install "guard rails" (heavy wooden poles or metal pipes placed 20cm out from the walls and 20cm off the floor) in farrowing pens.

tip43.title: Breeding Records Calendar
tip43.content: Mark the breeding date on a calendar; pregnancy lasts 114 days (3 months, 3 weeks, 3 days), allowing you to prepare the farrowing pen.

tip47.title: Gilt First Mating Age
tip47.content: Wait until a gilt is at least 8 months old and on her second or third heat cycle before mating her to ensure a larger first litter.

tip48.title: Isolation of New Purchases
tip48.content: Keep newly purchased pigs in a separate pen at least 20 meters away from your main herd for 30 days to check for sickness.

tip49.title: DIY Footbath Setup
tip49.content: Place a shallow plastic tub or half-cut tire filled with water and disinfectant (like chlorine or agricultural lime) at the farm gate.

tip51.title: Visitor Restriction Policy
tip51.content: Do not allow visitors (especially other pig buyers or farmers) inside your pig pens. Discuss business outside the pen area.

tip55.title: Natural Parasite Control
tip55.content: Feed crushed papaya seeds or pumpkin seeds to grower pigs as a cheap, natural remedy to help reduce internal worms.

tip60.title: Proper Burial of Mortalities
tip60.content: Bury dead pigs at least 2 meters deep, far away from water sources, and cover the carcass with agricultural lime before refilling the soil.

tip61.title: Low-Cost Pen Space
tip61.content: Provide at least 1 square meter of space per finisher pig to reduce fighting, stress, and skin lesions.

tip62.title: Natural Cross-Ventilation
tip62.content: Design pig houses with open sides (using wire mesh or bamboo slats) to allow natural breeze to blow through and remove odors.

tip75.title: DIY Compost Piles
tip75.content: Pile pig manure in a designated dry corner, cover it with banana leaves or plastic, and turn it weekly with a shovel to make compost.

tip76.title: Flexible Bag Biogas
tip76.content: Install a simple, low-cost plastic tubular biogas digester to treat pig manure, producing free cooking gas for the household.

tip92.title: Castration Age Limit
tip92.content: Castrate male piglets before they are 7 days old. At this age, the procedure is fast, heals quickly, and causes minimal pain.

tip94.title: Simple Notebook Records
tip94.content: Keep a small, cheap notebook in the barn to write down breeding dates, farrowing dates, and treatments for each sow.

Symptoms.title: Swine Disease Diagnostic Engine
Symptoms.description: Select the clinical signs observed in your pigs. The analyzer compares active symptoms against our reference database of swine illnesses.
Symptoms.analysisPrompt: Check observed symptoms and click "Analyze Symptoms" to run diagnostic search.
Symptoms.premiumDesc: Analyzing swine diseases is a Premium Feature. Upgraded farms gain full access to the AI diagnostics matcher, match accuracy indicators, and tailored biosecurity prevention guidelines.
Symptoms.noMatches: No matching diseases found for the selected symptoms.
Symptoms.noMatchesDesc: Please modify selection or consult your local veterinary officer for physical checks.
Symptoms.otherSymptoms: Other symptoms to watch out for:
Symptoms.disclaimer: Disclaimer: This AI tool is powered by veterinary research for educational assistance. It is NOT a substitute for professional veterinary diagnosis. Always consult a qualified veterinary practitioner before initiating treatments or purchasing medications.

dis_asf.name: African Swine Fever (ASF)
dis_asf.description: A highly contagious and deadly viral disease prevalent in West Africa. No vaccine exists.
dis_asf.prevention: Strict biosecurity and restrict farm access.

dis_fmd.name: Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD)
dis_fmd.description: Viral disease causing blisters on the mouth and feet.

dis_prrs.name: PRRS (Blue Ear)
dis_prrs.description: Causes respiratory failure and reproductive issues.

dis_erysipelas.name: Swine Erysipelas
dis_erysipelas.description: Bacterial infection causing distinct skin lesions and arthritis.

dis_csf.name: Classical Swine Fever
dis_csf.description: Highly contagious viral disease similar to ASF.

dis_anthrax.name: Anthrax
dis_anthrax.description: Serious bacterial disease that can affect humans.

dis_pneumonia.name: Mycoplasmal Pneumonia
dis_pneumonia.description: Chronic respiratory disease leading to poor growth.

dis_salmonellosis.name: Salmonellosis
dis_salmonellosis.description: Bacterial infection causing severe gut issues.

dis_ppv.name: Porcine Parvovirus (PPV)
dis_ppv.description: Common cause of reproductive failure in gilts.

dis_rhinitis.name: Atrophic Rhinitis
dis_rhinitis.description: Causes distortion of the snout and respiratory issues.

dis_edema.name: Edema Disease
dis_edema.description: Caused by E. coli toxins in weaned piglets.

dis_mange.name: Sarcoptic Mange
dis_mange.description: Parasitic skin disease causing extreme discomfort.

dis_anemia.name: Iron Deficiency Anemia
dis_anemia.description: Common in indoor piglets without iron supplements.

dis_ulcers.name: Gastric Ulcers
dis_ulcers.description: Caused by fine feed or stress.

dis_tge.name: Transmissible Gastroenteritis (TGE)
dis_tge.description: Viral disease with high mortality in young piglets.

dis_greasy_pig.name: Greasy Pig Disease
dis_greasy_pig.description: Bacterial infection causing greasy skin lesions.

dis_pseudorabies.name: Pseudorabies (Aujeszky's)
dis_pseudorabies.description: Viral disease affecting nervous and respiratory systems.

dis_pcv2.name: Porcine Circovirus (PCV2)
dis_pcv2.description: Causes wasting and respiratory issues in growers.

dis_ped.name: Porcine Epidemic Diarrhea (PED)
dis_ped.description: Similar to TGE, highly fatal to neonates.

dis_rabies.name: Rabies
dis_rabies.description: Fatal viral disease usually from bites.
dis_rabies.prevention: Vaccination of pets and wildlife control.

dis_pox.name: Swine Pox
dis_pox.description: Viral skin disease, often spread by lice.

dis_clostridial.name: Clostridial Enteritis
dis_clostridial.description: Bacterial infection causing severe gut damage.

dis_glassers.name: Glasser's Disease
dis_glassers.description: Causes inflammation of membranes around heart, lungs, joints.

dis_ileitis.name: Ileitis (Lawsonia)
dis_ileitis.description: Bacterial infection of the small intestine.

dis_tetanus.name: Tetanus
dis_tetanus.description: Bacterial toxin affecting the nervous system.

dis_ascaris.name: Ascaris suum
dis_ascaris.description: Internal parasite causing lung and liver damage.

dis_whipworms.name: Whipworms
dis_whipworms.description: Internal parasite in the large intestine.

dis_kidney_worms.name: Kidney Worms
dis_kidney_worms.description: Internal parasite affecting kidneys and liver.

dis_salt_poisoning.name: Salt Poisoning
dis_salt_poisoning.description: Caused by lack of water followed by over-consumption.

dis_heat_stroke.name: Heat Stroke
dis_heat_stroke.description: Overheating due to poor ventilation or shade.

dis_rectal_prolapse.name: Rectal Prolapse
dis_rectal_prolapse.description: Often follows coughing, diarrhea, or cold stress.

dis_splayleg.name: Splayleg
dis_splayleg.description: Piglets born unable to stand with hind legs.

dis_vulvovaginitis.name: Vulvovaginitis
dis_vulvovaginitis.description: Caused by estrogenic fungal toxins in feed.

dis_teschen.name: Teschen/Talfan Disease
dis_teschen.description: Viral infection of the nervous system.

dis_trypanosomiasis.name: Trypanosomiasis
dis_trypanosomiasis.description: Protozoan disease spread by tsetse flies.

dis_cysticercosis.name: Cysticercosis
dis_cysticercosis.description: Larval stage of human tapeworm in pig muscle.

dis_ear_necrosis.name: Ear Necrosis
dis_ear_necrosis.description: Often caused by stress, overcrowding, or infection.
dis_ear_necrosis.prevention: Reduce density and treat secondary infection.

dis_navel_ill.name: Navel Ill
dis_navel_ill.description: Infection of the umbilical cord at birth.
dis_navel_ill.prevention: Iodine dip at birth and sanitation.

dis_iron_toxicity.name: Iron Toxicity
dis_iron_toxicity.description: Excess iron administration leading to sudden death.

dis_aflatoxicosis.name: Aflatoxicosis
dis_aflatoxicosis.prevention: Avoid moldy feed and use toxin binders.

dis_parakeratosis.name: Parakeratosis
dis_parakeratosis.prevention: Ensure adequate zinc levels in diet.

dis_gossypol_toxicity.name: Gossypol Toxicity
dis_gossypol_toxicity.prevention: Limit raw cottonseed meal in feed.

dis_cassava_poisoning.name: Cassava Poisoning
dis_cassava_poisoning.prevention: Cook or ferment cassava before feeding.

dis_sweet_potato_toxicity.name: Sweet Potato Toxicity
dis_sweet_potato_toxicity.prevention: Do not feed moldy or damaged sweet potatoes.

dis_mma.name: MMA Syndrome
dis_mma.prevention: Clean farrowing pens and proper nutrition.

dis_trichinellosis.name: Trichinellosis
dis_trichinellosis.prevention: Do not feed raw garbage; pest control.

dis_hydatid_disease.name: Hydatid Disease
dis_hydatid_disease.prevention: Deworm farm dogs; prevent access to offal.

dis_phe.name: Proliferative Hemorrhagic Enteropathy
dis_phe.description: Acute intestinal bleeding in young adult pigs.

dis_foot_rot.name: Foot Rot
dis_foot_rot.description: Infection of the foot causing lameness.

dis_sow_hysteria.name: Sow Hysteria
dis_sow_hysteria.description: Aggression or panic in sows after farrowing.

dis_thorny_headed_worm.name: Thorny-Headed Worm
dis_thorny_headed_worm.prevention: Prevent access to June beetle grubs.

dis_fascioliasis.name: Fascioliasis
dis_fascioliasis.prevention: Snail control; keep away from wet pastures.

dis_photosensitization.name: Photosensitization
dis_photosensitization.prevention: Keep out of direct sunlight; check pasture plants.

dis_shoulder_ulcers.name: Shoulder Ulcers
dis_shoulder_ulcers.description: Sores in thin sows on hard floors.

dis_cold_stress.name: Cold Stress
dis_cold_stress.prevention: Provide heat lamps and dry bedding.

dis_navel_bleeding.name: Navel Bleeding
dis_navel_bleeding.prevention: Tie off bleeding navels; check feed vitamin K.

dis_ergotism.name: Ergotism
dis_ergotism.prevention: Inspect feed grains for black ergot bodies.

dis_bvdv_pig.name: BVDV in Pigs
dis_bvdv_pig.description: Bovine Viral Diarrhea virus in swine.

dis_congenital_tremor.name: Congenital Tremor
dis_congenital_tremor.prevention: Acclimatize gilts to farm before breeding.

s1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
s1.content: By accessing and using SmartSwine app, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use the service.

s2.title: 2. Description of Service
s2.content:
"SmartSwine" provides farm management tools including, but not limited to:
• Herd tracking and health data.
• Financial record keeping.
• Human Resource management.
• AI-assisted diagnostic suggestions.
• Market access information.

s3.title: 3. Data Privacy & Cloud Backup
s3.content:
You retain ownership of the data you input (herd data, financials, e.t.c.). 
Your farm data is stored securely using Google Firebase, while we strive to protect your data, we cannot guarantee absolute security. 
By enabling Cloud Backup, you consent to the storage and synchronization of your data across your devices. 
We do not sell your personal or farm data to third parties.
You are responsible for the accuracy of the data entered. Incorrect data in the "Feed" or "Financials" modules may result in incorrect calculations.

s4.title: 4. No Medical or Veterinary Advice (Critical Disclaimer)
s4.content:
The "Feed Calculator", "Feed Formulator", "Symptoms Analyzer" and "Disease Finder" features are for educational and informational purposes only.
• The AI results are generated by algorithms and are not a substitute for professional veterinary advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
• The User agrees to consult a licensed veterinarian before making any feeding and medical decisions regarding livestock.
• The App Owner is not liable for any livestock loss or damages resulting from the use of the app and its features.

s5.title: 5. User Responsibilities
s5.content: Users are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of their account credentials. You are responsible for all activities that occur under your account.

s6.title: 6. Prohibited Conduct
s6.content:
Users agree not to:
• Use the app for any illegal purposes.
• Attempt to reverse engineer the application.
• Input fraudulent financial data for the purpose of deceiving third parties.

s7.title: 7. Service Availability
s7.content: We strive for 99% uptime but do not guarantee uninterrupted access to the service. Data backups are provided as a convenience; however, users are encouraged to maintain their own records.

s8.title: 8. Limitation of Liability
s8.content: SmartSwine app is provided "as is." To the maximum extent permitted by law, its developers shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential damages resulting from the use or inability to use the service, including livestock loss or financial discrepancies.

s9.title: 9. Financial Data
s9.content: The "Financials" module is a tracking tool and does not constitute professional accounting or tax advice. Users are encouraged to export their data for review by certified accountants.

s10.title: 10. Changes to Terms
s10.content: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the app constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

s11.title: 11. Contact Information
s11.content: For support or legal inquiries, please contact the developer through the app's profile settings or at bibiniitech@gmail.com.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.importantTitle: Your Data Privacy is Important to Us
Privacy.importantDesc: SmartSwine is committed to protecting your personal and farm data. We use industry-standard security measures to ensure your information is kept safe.
Privacy.dataOwnershipDesc: You retain full ownership of all data you input into SmartSwine, including herd records, financials, and staff details.
Privacy.cloudSecurityDesc: Your data is stored securely using Google Firebase, featuring encrypted storage and synchronization across your devices.
Privacy.noSalesDesc: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal or farm data to third parties for marketing purposes.
Privacy.accuracyDesc: You are responsible for the accuracy of the data entered. Correct data ensures precise AI diagnostic and feed calculations.

Settings.autoSowsDesc: Automatically set status to Sow after first farrowing.
Settings.giltDesc: Age threshold for classifying a female as a Gilt if she hasn't farrowed.
Settings.clearHerdDesc: Delete all pig profiles and health records. Use with caution.
Settings.clearFinDesc: Delete all income and expense records.
Settings.clearFeedDesc: Delete all feed inventory and transactions.
Settings.clearHRDesc: Delete all staff and payroll records.
Settings.cloudSyncDesc: Your data is automatically synchronized with the SmartSwine Cloud via Firebase.
Settings.aboutDesc: Designed for desktop access to your farm data. SmartSwine provides advanced herd tracking, feed formulation, and financial analytics for modern pig farmers.
